conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To continue assessing your eligibility, I need to ask:

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

Do you have the right to reside in the UK? (For example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, pre-settled status, or another immigration status that gives you the right to live in the UK?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have the right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about Alex's living arrangements:

Does Alex live with you full-time, or do they split their time between you and someone else (such as another parent or guardian)?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A couple more questions:

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? (This would be relevant if Alex were 16 or older, but I want to confirm there are no special circumstances like being in hospital or care.)

Also, is there anyone else who might also be claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
No, Alex is only 8, so they're just in primary school. 

However, Alex has been in hospital for a while now - about three months. I'm still looking after them though, and I visit regularly.

And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have one more important question about Alex's hospital stay:

When Alex is expected to leave hospital, will they be returning to live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex will be coming back home to live with me once they're discharged.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements:
- You are responsible for Alex who is 8 years old (under 16)
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- Alex normally lives with you full-time
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**Regarding Alex's hospital stay:** Although Alex has been in hospital for three months (which is longer than the initial 12-week period), you can continue to claim Child Benefit because Alex is expected to return to your care when they leave hospital. The rules allow you to keep claiming after the 12-week period if the child will be returning to live with you.

You should be able to apply for or continue claiming Child Benefit without any issues. I hope Alex recovers soon and is back home with you quickly.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives in the UK, and has the right to reside in the UK. Alex normally lives with the claimant full-time, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Although Alex has been in hospital for three months, according to the rules at https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit-for-children-in-hospital-or-care, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for up to 12 weeks (84 days) while a child is in hospital. After 12 weeks, you can still claim if the child is expected to be in your care when they leave hospital or be in your care within 12 weeks of leaving hospital. Since Alex will be returning to live with the claimant when discharged, the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet all the basic eligibility criteria: they are responsible for Alex, live in the UK with the right to reside, Alex lives with them full-time, and nobody else is claiming for Alex. Although Alex has been in hospital for three months (exceeding the initial 12-week period), the claimant can continue to claim Child Benefit because Alex is expected to return to their care upon discharge from hospital.
